mechanism_narrative: Non-farm payrolls trending higher reflects persistent labor market expansion that drives aggregate income and consumption, lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and SPX 20-day forward returns turns negative or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds restrictive thresholds, equity drawdown probability rises as borrowing costs constrain corporate activity and equity risk premiums expand, producing negative sixty-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.25%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and retail sales signals a synchronized downturn in manufacturing and consumer demand, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns that neither proxy captures independently.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to produce negative average 20-day SPX returns significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during a late-cycle expansion reduces expected corporate earnings growth and triggers risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals improving operating leverage and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO acceleration does not correlate with positive 5-day SPX forward returns; rolling 1-year Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below its twelve-month moving average indicates collapsing residential investment and upstream demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below its 12-month moving average coincides with positive average 20-day SPX returns (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated CPI inflation and a restrictive federal funds rate above neutral creates a stagflationary impulse where discount rates rise while profit margins compress, generating negative S&P 500 returns over sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of FEDFUNDS above 2.0% and CPIAUCSL YoY above 2.5% does not produce negative average 60-day SPX returns, or the interaction term is insignificant in a panel regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth changes correlate with persistent demand-driven earnings revisions, producing positive directional S&P 500 returns over the subsequent twenty trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly changes fail to predict positive 20-day SPX forward returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
